stories.title: View and Manage Requirements Mapping List in Backoffice
stories.as_a: user with access to PICASso content in Backoffice
stories.i_want: to view, filter, and manage the Requirements Mapping list on a dedicated page under Product Requirements
stories.so_that: I can oversee all requirement mapping pairs and activate or deactivate them as needed
acceptance_criteria: A new page 'Requirements Mapping' is available under the 'Product Requirements' section in the Backoffice navigation. (see Figma [Requirements Mapping – Empty State|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1792-58241])

The page contains the following filters above the table:
  - Search field — allows searching by Requirement Code
  - Standard — single-select dropdown listing all standards available in the system
  - Linked Standard — single-select dropdown listing all standards available in the system
  - Show Inactive — toggle button (default: unchecked, showing only active mappings; when checked, both active and inactive mappings are displayed) (see Figma [Requirements Mapping – Show Inactive On|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1792-65573])

The table displays the following columns:
  - Standard — name of the standard/policy related to the requirement
  - Requirement — clickable code(s) of the requirement/sub-requirement; clicking redirects to the Edit Requirement/Sub-requirement page accordingly
  - Linked Standard — name of the standard/policy related to the linked requirement
  - Linked Requirement — clickable code(s) of the linked requirement/sub-requirement; clicking redirects to the Edit Requirement/Sub-requirement page accordingly
  - Is Active — green checkmark when active, grey checkmark when inactive
  - Actions — three-dots menu with options: Edit, and either 'Activate' or 'Deactivate' depending on the current status of the pair (see Figma [Requirements Mapping – Show Inactive Off|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1794-89262])

When the user selects 'Deactivate' from the Actions menu for an active mapping pair, a confirmation pop-up is shown with the text: 'Are you sure you want to deactivate?' and two options — 'Cancel' (closes the pop-up, pair remains active) and 'Ok'. (see Figma [Requirement Mapping – Deactivate Mapping|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1792-58581])

When the user clicks 'Ok' on the deactivation pop-up, the system deactivates the mapping pair, shows a success message at the top of the screen: 'Requirements pair <Requirement> to <Linked requirement> is successfully deactivated', updates the pair status to Inactive, and the pair is no longer considered for the release traceability view.

When the user selects 'Activate' from the Actions menu for an inactive mapping pair, a confirmation pop-up is shown with the text: 'Are you sure you want to activate?' and two options — 'Cancel' (closes the pop-up, pair remains inactive) and 'Ok'.

When the user clicks 'Ok' on the activation pop-up, the system activates the mapping pair, shows a success message: 'Requirements pair <Requirement> to <Linked requirement> is successfully activated', updates the pair status to Active, and the pair becomes available for the release traceability view.

The table on the empty state (no mapping pairs yet) displays an appropriate empty state message. (see Figma [Requirement Mapping – Empty State|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1792-58241])

Filtering by Standard narrows the list to mapping pairs where the Standard column matches the selected value. Filtering by Linked Standard narrows results to matching Linked Standard values. Both filters can be applied simultaneously.

The 'Edit Selection' state shows when the user selects mappings for bulk operations if applicable. (see Figma [Requirements Mapping – Edit Selection|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1792-58425])

confluence_links.label: Requirements Traceability within Standards – Requirements Mapping page

stories.source_requirement: Backoffice → Requirements Mapping page → View list of requirements mapping pairs; Activating/deactivating requirements mapping pair

stories.title: Add and Edit Requirements Mapping Pair in Backoffice
stories.as_a: user with access to PICASso content in Backoffice
stories.i_want: to add new requirement mapping pairs or edit existing ones on dedicated pages
stories.so_that: I can define and maintain traceability relationships between requirements of different standards
acceptance_criteria: On the 'Requirements Mapping' page, clicking the 'Add Requirements Mapping Pair' button opens a new 'New Requirement Mapping Pair' page. (see Figma [Requirement Mapping – New Mapping – Default|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1792-58908])

Clicking 'Edit' from the Actions menu of an existing mapping pair opens the 'Edit Requirement Mapping Pair' page. (see Figma [Requirements Mapping – Edit|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1792-59039])

Both Add and Edit pages contain the following fields:
  - Standard — dropdown, single select, mandatory — lists all standards available in the system
  - Requirement — dropdown, multi-select, mandatory — activated only after a Standard is selected; shows only requirements/sub-requirements related to the selected standard
  - Linked Standard — dropdown with free-text typing, mandatory — allows selecting an existing standard or typing a new one; lists all standards available in the system
  - Linked Requirement — dropdown, multi-select, mandatory — activated only after Linked Standard is selected; shows only requirements/sub-requirements related to the selected linked standard
  - Is Active — checkbox (checked by default) — when checked, the mapping is active; when unchecked, the mapping is inactive

The Requirement dropdown is disabled/inactive until a Standard is selected. Once a Standard is selected, the Requirement dropdown activates and shows only requirements and sub-requirements belonging to that standard.

The Linked Requirement dropdown is disabled/inactive until a Linked Standard is selected. Once Linked Standard is selected, the Linked Requirement dropdown activates and shows only requirements and sub-requirements belonging to that linked standard.

The Linked Standard field supports free-text typing to allow creating a new standard name not yet in the system, in addition to selecting from existing standards. (see Figma [Requirement Mapping – Linked Standard – Focus|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1793-68878])

When the user clicks 'Save' and at least one mandatory field is empty, the system shows an error 'Please fill in the required fields' at the top of the screen, and each empty mandatory field is highlighted in red with the message 'Required Field!'. (see Figma [Requirement Mapping – New Mapping – Errors|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1792-59069])

When all mandatory fields are filled and the user clicks 'Save', the system saves the mapping pair, shows a success message — 'Requirement mapping pair is successfully added.' for new pairs or 'Requirement mapping pair is successfully updated.' for edits — and redirects the user to the Requirements Mapping page.

After saving, the new or updated mapping pair is propagated to all versions of the requirements included in the mapping pair. If requirements were removed during an edit, they are also removed from all versions of those requirements.

Clicking the 'Back' button on the New or Edit page discards all unsaved changes and returns the user to the Requirements Mapping page without saving anything. (see Figma [Requirement Mapping – New Mapping – Filled|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1793-68386])

A tooltip is shown when hovering over the Linked Requirement field explaining multi-value behavior. (see Figma [Requirements Mapping – Linked Requirement – Tooltip|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1849-16822])

confluence_links.label: Requirements Traceability within Standards – Add/edit requirements mapping pair

stories.source_requirement: Backoffice → Requirements Mapping page → Add/edit requirements mapping pair

stories.title: Download and Upload Requirements Mapping File in Backoffice
stories.as_a: user with access to PICASso content in Backoffice
stories.i_want: to download and upload requirements mapping pairs via Excel files
stories.so_that: I can bulk-manage traceability mappings outside the UI and import changes in bulk
acceptance_criteria:
On the 'Requirements Mapping' page, a 'Download Requirements Mapping' button is available. Clicking it exports an .xlsx file with all mapping pairs currently in the system, with the following columns:
  - Standard — name of the standard/policy
  - Requirement — requirement/sub-requirement codes (multiple values comma-separated)
  - Linked Standard — name of the linked standard/policy
  - Linked Requirement — linked requirement/sub-requirement codes (multiple values comma-separated)
  - Is Active — 'True' for active pairs, 'False' for inactive pairs

A 'Template Download' button is also available on the page. Clicking it exports an empty .xlsx file with the same column structure and the following data types:
  - Standard (string)
  - Requirement (string; multiple values separated by comma)
  - Linked Standard (string)
  - Linked Requirement (string; multiple values separated by comma)
  - Is Active (boolean: True or False; empty value is treated as 'True')
A 'Select file' button allows users to upload a completed template file. Clicking it opens the system file picker restricted to .xlsx files.

Upon file upload, the system validates the file and row data according to the following rules:
  - Standard empty → 'Standard column can't be empty Lines <line number>'
  - Standard value not found in system → 'Standard does not exist in the system Lines <line number>'
  - Requirement empty → 'Requirement column can't be empty Lines <line number>'
  - Requirement value not found or codes separated by symbol other than comma → 'Requirement does not exist in the system. Several codes can be separated only by comma. Lines <line number>'
  - Linked Standard empty → 'Linked standard column can't be empty Lines <line number>'
  - Linked Standard not found in system → 'Linked standard does not exist in the system Lines <line number>'
  - Linked Requirement empty → 'Linked requirement column can't be empty Lines <line number>'
  - Linked Requirement not found or wrong separator → 'Linked requirement does not exist in the system. Several codes can be separated only by comma. Lines <line number>'
  - Is Active contains unsupported value (not True, False, or empty) → 'Is Active status contains unsupported value (can be only "True" or "False") Lines <line number>'
  - Wrong file format (not .xlsx) → 'Please upload valid file type (.xlsx)'
  - Column structure does not match template → 'Columns structure is incorrect, please use the columns from the template file.'
All validation errors are shown to the user with the corresponding line numbers. Multiple errors may be shown at once if multiple rows contain issues.

If all validations pass, the system shows a success message: 'File was uploaded successfully', and the uploaded/updated mapping pairs are propagated to all versions of the requirements included in each mapping pair. If previously existing requirements were removed by the upload, they are also removed from all versions of those requirements.
If the file has the wrong format (.csv, .doc, etc.), the system rejects the file immediately and shows the error 'Please upload valid file type (.xlsx)' without further processing.

If the column structure of the uploaded file does not match the template (e.g., renamed or missing columns), the system shows the error 'Columns structure is incorrect, please use the columns from the template file.' and does not process any rows.

confluence_links.label: Requirements Traceability within Standards – Updating requirements mapping with file upload

stories.source_requirement: Backoffice → Requirements Mapping page → Updating requirements mapping with the file upload

stories.title: [In Question] Requirements Mapping Tab on Edit Requirement Page
stories.as_a: user with access to PICASso content in Backoffice
stories.i_want: to see a 'Requirements Mapping' tab on the Edit Product Requirement/Sub-requirement page showing all linked standards and requirements for this requirement
stories.so_that: I can quickly review all traceability mappings for a specific requirement without leaving its edit page
acceptance_criteria: A new tab named 'Requirements Mapping' is added to the 'Edit Product Requirement' and 'Edit Product Sub-requirement' pages in the Backoffice.

The 'Requirements Mapping' tab contains the following filters:
  - Search field — allows searching by Requirement Code
  - Standard — dropdown listing all standards available in the system
  - Show Inactive toggle — when unchecked (default), only active mappings are shown; when checked, both active and inactive mappings are displayed

The tab displays a table with the following columns for all requirements linked to the current requirement:
  - Linked Standard — name of the standard/policy related to the linked requirement
  - Linked Requirement — clickable code(s) of the linked requirement/sub-requirement; clicking redirects to the Edit Requirement/Sub-requirement page for that linked requirement
  - Is Active — green checkmark when active, grey checkmark when inactive
The tab is read-only — no add/edit/delete actions are available directly from this tab. Mapping management is performed on the 'Requirements Mapping' page.
If no mappings exist for this requirement, the tab shows an appropriate empty state.
Filtering by Standard narrows the list to mappings where the Standard matches the selected value. The Show Inactive toggle works the same way as on the Requirements Mapping page.
This feature is 'In Question' — implementation is subject to confirmation from the product team before development begins.

confluence_links.label: Requirements Traceability within Standards – Requirements Mapping tab on Edit Requirements page

stories.source_requirement: Backoffice → Requirements Mapping tab on the Edit Requirements page (In question if we need it)

stories.title: Manage Standards in Backoffice (View, Add, Edit, Activate/Deactivate)
stories.as_a: user with access to PICASso content in Backoffice
stories.i_want: to manage the list of product requirement standards on a dedicated 'Standards' page under Product Requirements
stories.so_that: I can define, update, and control which standards are available for requirements mapping and source configurations
acceptance_criteria: A new page 'Standards' is available under the 'Product Requirements' section in the Backoffice navigation. (see Figma [Standards – Empty State|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1851-21033])

The Standards page contains the following filters:
  - Search field — allows searching by Standard Name or Code
  - Show Inactive toggle — unchecked by default (only active standards shown); when checked, both active and inactive are displayed (see Figma [Standards – Show Inactive On|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1851-22355])

The table on the Standards page has the following columns:
  - Code — unique code of the standard
  - Standard — name of the standard/policy; the value is clickable and opens the 'Edit Standard' page
  - Is Active — green checkmark when active, grey checkmark when inactive
  - Actions — three-dots menu with options: Edit, and either 'Activate' or 'Deactivate' depending on the current status (see Figma [Standards – Show Inactive Off|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1851-21740])

On the Standards page, clicking 'Add Standard' opens the 'New Standard' page. Clicking a standard's name or selecting 'Edit' from the Actions menu opens the 'Edit Standard' page. (see Figma [New Standard – Default|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1851-21131])

The New Standard and Edit Standard pages contain the following fields:
  - Code — string, mandatory — unique code for the standard
  - Standard — string, mandatory — name of the standard displayed in configurations
  - Is Active — checkbox (checked by default) — when checked, standard is active; when unchecked, standard is inactive

When the user clicks 'Save' on the New/Edit Standard page:
  - If the Code is already used by another standard, an error is shown: 'Specified Code is already used for other standard. Please check and update the value' at the top, and the Code field is highlighted in red with the message 'Code should be unique'. (see Figma [New Standard – Code Already Used Error|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1851-21527])
  - If at least one mandatory field is empty, an error is shown: 'Please fill in the required fields' at the top, and each empty field is highlighted in red with 'Required Field!'. (see Figma [New Standard – Errors|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1851-24524])
  - If all validations pass, a success message is shown: 'Standard is successfully added.' or 'Standard is successfully updated.', the user is redirected to the Standards page, and the Sources page reflects the updated standard immediately.
Clicking 'Back' on the New or Edit Standard page discards all unsaved changes and returns the user to the Standards page without saving.

Selecting 'Deactivate' from the Actions menu for an active standard shows a confirmation pop-up: 'Are you sure you want to deactivate?' with 'Cancel' and 'Ok'. Clicking 'Ok' deactivates the standard, shows the success message: 'Standard <Standard name> is successfully deactivated', updates the status to Inactive, and prevents the standard from being selected in Sources when editing. (see Figma [Standards – Deactivate Standard|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1851-23209])

Selecting 'Activate' from the Actions menu for an inactive standard shows a confirmation pop-up: 'Are you sure you want to activate?' with 'Cancel' and 'Ok'. Clicking 'Ok' activates the standard, shows the success message: 'Standard <Standard name> is successfully activated', updates the status to Active, and makes the standard selectable in Sources when editing.

The Standards page shows an empty state when no standards have been created yet. (see Figma [Standards – Edit Selection|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1851-22600])
The New Standard page shows the filled state correctly when data is entered. (see Figma [New Standard – Filled|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1851-21342])

The Edit Standard page is pre-populated with the existing values of the selected standard. (see Figma [Standard – Edit|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1851-22835])

mockups.label: New Standard – Code Already Used Error

confluence_links.label: Requirements Traceability within Standards – Standard page for Product Requirements

stories.source_requirement: Backoffice → 'Standard' page for Product Requirements (View list of standards; Add/edit standard; Activating/deactivating standard)

stories.title: Add Standard Field to Requirements Sources in Backoffice
stories.as_a: user with access to PICASso content in Backoffice
stories.i_want: to see and set the Standard field on the Requirements Sources pages and list
stories.so_that: I can associate a standard with each source and keep the Source List aligned with current configurations
acceptance_criteria: A new 'Standard' column is added to the 'Source List' page (Backoffice → Product Requirements → Source). The column displays the name of the Standard associated with that source as a string. (see Figma [Source List – Default|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1792-58270])
The columns 'Is Security Level' and 'Is Regional' are removed from the Source List page.

A new optional dropdown field 'Standard' is added to both the 'New Source' and 'Edit Source' pages. The dropdown lists all active standards from the Priorities LoV (i.e., standards that are currently active on the Standards page). The field is optional — a source can be saved without selecting a standard.
The fields 'Is Security Level' and 'Is Regional' are removed from the 'New Source' and 'Edit Source' pages.

When a standard is selected from the dropdown and the source is saved, the Standard column on the Source List page displays the selected standard name. (see Figma [Source List – New Source – Standard – Focus|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1849-17173])
When no standard is selected for a source, the Standard column on the Source List displays an empty value for that row.

The Standard dropdown on the Source pages reflects only active standards. If a standard is deactivated after being assigned to a source, it remains displayed for existing sources that already have it assigned, but cannot be selected for new or edited sources.
All other existing fields and behaviors on the Source List, New Source, and Edit Source pages remain unchanged.

Error handling on the New Source and Edit Source pages remains intact for other mandatory fields. (see Figma [Source List – New Source – Errors|https://www.figma.com/design/VCnhNo2gacNBDgTeyHRRYB/Backoffice?node-id=1797-89937])

mockups.label: Source List – New Source – Standard – Focus

confluence_links.label: Requirements Traceability within Standards – Add Standard field for Requirements Sources page

stories.source_requirement: Backoffice → Add 'Standard' field for 'Requirements Sources' page

stories.title: Manage Priorities in Backoffice (View, Add, Edit, Activate/Deactivate)
stories.as_a: user with access to PICASso content in Backoffice
stories.i_want: to manage the list of requirement priorities on a dedicated 'Priorities' page under Product Requirements
stories.so_that: I can define, update, and control which priorities are available for product requirements and sub-requirements
acceptance_criteria: A new page 'Priorities' is available under the 'Product Requirements' section in the Backoffice navigation.

The Priorities page contains the following filters:
  - Search field — allows searching by Priority Name or Code
  - Show Inactive toggle — unchecked by default (only active priorities shown); when checked, both active and inactive are displayed

The table on the Priorities page has the following columns:
  - Code — unique code of the priority
  - Priority — name of the priority; the value is clickable and opens the 'Edit Priority' page
  - Is Active — green checkmark when active, grey checkmark when inactive
  - Actions — three-dots menu with options: Edit, and either 'Activate' or 'Deactivate' depending on current status

Clicking 'Add Priority' opens the 'New Priority' page. Clicking on a priority name or selecting 'Edit' from Actions opens the 'Edit Priority' page. Note: the spec states 'New Standard' page is opened — this appears to be a copy-paste error; the correct page should be 'New Priority'.

The New Priority and Edit Priority pages contain the following fields:
  - Code — string, mandatory — unique code for the priority
  - Priority — string, mandatory — name of the priority displayed in configurations
  - Is Active — checkbox (checked by default) — when checked, priority is active; when unchecked, priority is inactive

When the user clicks 'Save':
  - If the Code is already used by another priority, an error is shown: 'Specified Code is already used for other priority. Please check and update the value' at the top, and the Code field is highlighted in red with 'Code should be unique'.
  - If at least one mandatory field is empty, an error is shown: 'Please fill in the required fields' at the top, and each empty field is highlighted with 'Required Field!'.
  - If all validations pass, a success message is shown: 'Priority is successfully added.' or 'Priority is successfully updated.', the user is redirected to the Priorities page, and the corresponding priority is updated for any existing Product Requirements/sub-requirements it was already assigned to.
Clicking 'Back' discards all unsaved changes and returns to the Priorities page.

Selecting 'Deactivate' for an active priority shows a confirmation pop-up: 'Are you sure you want to deactivate?' with 'Cancel' and 'Ok'. Clicking 'Ok' deactivates the priority, shows: 'Priority <Priority name> is successfully deactivated', updates status to Inactive. A deactivated priority cannot be selected when editing Product Requirements, Sub-requirements, or Custom Requirements, but remains visible in read-only views and exported files (both Backoffice and Frontoffice).

Selecting 'Activate' for an inactive priority shows a confirmation pop-up: 'Are you sure you want to activate?' with 'Cancel' and 'Ok'. Clicking 'Ok' activates the priority, shows: 'Priority <Priority name> is successfully activated', updates status to Active, and the priority becomes selectable again for Product Requirements/Sub-requirements/Custom Requirements.

The existing 'Must' and 'Should' values from the previous 'Must Or Should' field are added as the first two values in the new Priorities page. All existing requirements retain their assigned 'Must' or 'Should' value; only the field/column name is changed to 'Priority'.
For all existing products, the current 'Must Or Should' values on requirements remain unchanged when the rename is applied.

confluence_links.label: Requirements Traceability within Standards – Priority page for Product Requirements

stories.source_requirement: Backoffice → 'Priority' page for Product Requirements (View list of priorities; Add/edit priority; Activating/deactivating priority)

stories.title: Rename 'Must Or Should' to 'Priority' Across the System
stories.as_a: user with access to PICASso content in Backoffice and Frontoffice
stories.i_want: to see the 'Must Or Should' field and column renamed to 'Priority' everywhere it appears in the system
stories.so_that: the field name aligns with the new Priorities management configuration and provides consistent terminology
acceptance_criteria:
The following pages in Backoffice must display 'Priority' instead of 'Must Or Should':
  - 'New Product Requirement' and 'Edit Product Requirement' pages — field label updated to 'Priority'
  - 'New Product Sub-Requirement' and 'Edit Product Sub-Requirement' pages — field label updated to 'Priority'
  - Product Requirement export file — column header updated to 'Priority'
  - Product Requirement template file — column header updated to 'Priority'
  - Product Sub-Requirement export file — column header updated to 'Priority'
  - Product Sub-Requirement template file — column header updated to 'Priority'

The following locations in Frontoffice must display 'Priority' instead of 'Must Or Should':
  - 'Add Custom Requirement' pop-up — field label updated to 'Priority'
  - 'Edit Custom Requirement' pop-up — field label updated to 'Priority'
  - Column header on the 'Product Requirements' page in a release — updated to 'Priority'
  - Product Requirement export file in the release — column header updated to 'Priority'

The field name in the POST /requirements API used to send requirements to Jama remains unchanged as 'MustOrShould'. New priority values added via the Priorities page in Backoffice are sent in this 'MustOrShould' API field using their configured names.
For product requirement updates in Backoffice, changes to the 'Priority' field/column continue to be classified as a 'Minor' change (not a Major change).
All existing requirements that have a value in 'Must Or Should' retain their values after the rename. No data migration is required — only the display label changes to 'Priority'.
The Priorities dropdown in the UI (Backoffice and Frontoffice) is populated from the active priorities available in the Priorities LoV, not hardcoded to 'Must'/'Should' only.
Existing 'Must' and 'Should' values remain selectable as priority options (they are added as the first two entries on the Priorities page).

confluence_links.label: Requirements Traceability within Standards – Update 'Must Or Should' field to 'Priority'

stories.source_requirement: Backoffice + Frontoffice → Update 'Must Or Should' field and column across the system to 'Priority'

stories.title: Set Default Requirements Traceability View on Product Details
stories.as_a: user with privilege to create and edit Products in PICASso
stories.i_want: to configure the default requirements traceability view for a product on the Product Configuration sub-tab
stories.so_that: all releases of this product open the Product Requirements page with the correct traceability perspective pre-selected
acceptance_criteria: A new dropdown field 'Default Requirements traceability view' is added to the 'Product Configuration' sub-tab on the Product Details page. (see Figma [Product Configuration – Default Requirements Traceability View – Default|https://www.figma.com/design/MB19XU18rQHSpba8pSsObn/Release?node-id=6789-74518])

The dropdown options are:
  - 'All requirements' (default option, always present)
  - All active bi-directional combinations of standards mapping pairs configured in Backoffice (e.g., if CTI/CTS ↔ IEC62443 mapping exists: 'CTI/CTS → IEC62443' and 'IEC62443 → CTI/CTS' are both listed)

The dropdown dynamically reflects the currently active mapping pairs from the Backoffice 'Requirements Mapping' page. Adding or deactivating a mapping pair in Backoffice is reflected in this dropdown for all products.

For example, with the following active mapping pairs:
  - CTI/CTS ↔ IEC62443
  - IEC62443 ↔ OWASP
  - CTI/CTS ↔ OWASP
  The dropdown shows: All Requirements, CTI/CTS → IEC62443, IEC62443 → CTI/CTS, IEC62443 → OWASP, OWASP → IEC62443, CTI/CTS → OWASP, OWASP → CTI/CTS. (see Figma [Product Configuration – Default Requirements Traceability View – Filled|https://www.figma.com/design/MB19XU18rQHSpba8pSsObn/Release?node-id=6789-75558])

The value selected in 'Default Requirements traceability view' is applied automatically when any release of this product opens the Product Requirements page — the Traceability view in the Settings dropdown on that page reflects the configured default.
For all existing products, this field is automatically set to 'All requirements' (no change to existing behavior). No manual migration is required.
When 'All requirements' is selected, the Product Requirements page in releases behaves as it currently does — no traceability grouping applied.

When a specific mapping pair is selected as default, the Product Requirements page in all releases of this product opens with that traceability view already active, without the user needing to manually select it each time.

mockups.label: Product Configuration – Default Requirements Traceability View – Default
Product Configuration – Default Requirements Traceability View – Filled

confluence_links.label: Requirements Traceability within Standards – Default view of requirements traceability on Product Details

stories.source_requirement: Frontoffice → Default view of requirements traceability on Product Details

stories.title: Update Action Toolbar to Icons on Product and Process Requirements Pages
stories.as_a: user with access to the Product Requirements or Process Requirements page in a release
stories.i_want: to see the actions toolbar replaced with icons displayed in the top-right corner above the table header
stories.so_that: the page is cleaner and I can access all relevant actions from a compact, consistent toolbar
acceptance_criteria: The buttons and toggles currently displayed on the Product Requirements and Process Requirements pages in a release are replaced by icon buttons in the top-right corner above the table header. (see Figma [Product Requirements – Default|https://www.figma.com/design/MB19XU18rQHSpba8pSsObn/Release?node-id=6560-46046])

The following icons are available in the toolbar (displayed based on applicable context):
  - **Refresh Jama/Jira icon** — shown only when requirements have been submitted to Jama or Jira. Displays the message 'Last update from Jira <date and time>' or 'Last update from Jama <date and time>'. Shows an error icon with a hover message if the last update failed. (see Figma [Product Requirements List – Submitted to Jira|https://www.figma.com/design/MB19XU18rQHSpba8pSsObn/Release?node-id=7171-56207])
  - **Diagrams icon** — opens the 'Requirement Status Summary' pop-up when clicked. (see Figma [Requirement Status Summary – Popup|https://www.figma.com/design/MB19XU18rQHSpba8pSsObn/Release?node-id=7193-92782])
  - **Settings icon** — opens a submenu with toggles (see below)
  - **Import/Export icon** — opens a submenu with import and export options (see below)

The Settings submenu (opened by clicking the Settings icon) shows different toggles depending on the page:
  - **Process Requirements page:**
    - Show Sub-Requirements (shown only if sub-requirements are visible on the page) — expands all sub-requirements
    - Show All Requirements — shows all requirements including those not in scope
  - **Product Requirements page:**
    - Show Sub-Requirements — expands all sub-requirements
    - Show All Requirements — shows all requirements including those not in scope
    - Traceability view — dropdown to select the active standards mapping pair for this release (see Figma [Product Requirements – Settings Submenu Opened|https://www.figma.com/design/MB19XU18rQHSpba8pSsObn/Release?node-id=6560-46348])
The Settings tooltip is shown on hover above the Settings icon. (see Figma [Product Requirements – Settings Tooltip|https://www.figma.com/design/MB19XU18rQHSpba8pSsObn/Release?node-id=6774-59363])

The Import/Export submenu (opened by clicking the Import/Export icon) shows two options:
  - **Import XLSX** — only shown during Scoping, Scope Approval, and Manage stages; allows importing a file to update requirement statuses or create new custom requirements. Import progress and a link to the error pop-up are shown below the icon.
  - **Export XLSX** — always visible; exports the list of requirements, statuses, and delegation details. For users with view-only privilege or on stages where statuses cannot be edited, clicking Export immediately starts the file download. (see Figma [Product Requirements – Import/Export XLSX Submenu Opened|https://www.figma.com/design/MB19XU18rQHSpba8pSsObn/Release?node-id=6560-47724])

The Import/Export tooltip is shown on hover above the Import/Export icon. (see Figma [Product Requirements – Import/Export XLSX Tooltip|https://www.figma.com/design/MB19XU18rQHSpba8pSsObn/Release?node-id=6774-60841])

When a file is loading after import, the loading state is shown below the icon. (see Figma [Product Requirements – Loading File Message|https://www.figma.com/design/MB19XU18rQHSpba8pSsObn/Release?node-id=6852-62044])

When an import file has errors, the error message and link to error details are shown. (see Figma [Product Requirements – File Error Message|https://www.figma.com/design/MB19XU18rQHSpba8pSsObn/Release?node-id=7275-176390])

When a Jira/Jama sync error has occurred, the error icon and hover message are displayed in the toolbar. (see Figma [Product Requirements – Jira Error Message|https://www.figma.com/design/MB19XU18rQHSpba8pSsObn/Release?node-id=7172-57526])

The Refresh Jira data tooltip is shown on hover above the Refresh icon. (see Figma [Product Requirements – Refresh Jira Data Tooltip|https://www.figma.com/design/MB19XU18rQHSpba8pSsObn/Release?node-id=6774-60102])

Process Requirements page also gains the 'Submitted to Jira' toolbar state. (see Figma [Process Requirements List – Submitted to Jira|https://www.figma.com/design/MB19XU18rQHSpba8pSsObn/Release?node-id=7335-203980])

confluence_links.label: Requirements Traceability within Standards – Updating buttons to icons on Product Requirements and Process Requirements pages

stories.source_requirement: Frontoffice → Updating buttons to icons on Product Requirements and Process Requirements pages

stories.title: Select Requirements Traceability View in Release
stories.as_a: user with access to the Product Requirements page in a release
stories.i_want: to select a requirements traceability view from the Settings submenu on the Product Requirements page
stories.so_that: I can visualize linked requirements from two standards side-by-side in a hierarchical layout and filter by linkage status
acceptance_criteria: In the Settings submenu of the Product Requirements page, a 'Traceability view' dropdown is shown. It lists all active standards mapping pairs configured in Backoffice (e.g., 'IEC62443 → CTI/CTS', 'CTI/CTS → IEC62443', etc.) plus the default option 'All requirements'. (see Figma [Product Requirements – Settings Submenu Opened – Traceability view – Focus|https://www.figma.com/design/MB19XU18rQHSpba8pSsObn/Release?node-id=6774-55004])

When a specific mapping pair is selected in 'Traceability view', the requirements list is refreshed to display requirements in the following hierarchy:
  1. Parent requirement from the 1st Standard in the selected pair
  2. Linked requirement(s) from the 2nd Standard (if any) — displayed below the parent, highlighted in blue with a chain icon in front
  3. Sub-requirement(s) of the parent from the 1st Standard
  4. Linked requirement(s) from the 2nd Standard for each sub-requirement (if any) — also highlighted in blue with a chain icon (see Figma [Product Requirements – IEC → CTI View Applied|https://www.figma.com/design/MB19XU18rQHSpba8pSsObn/Release?node-id=6709-5738] and [Product Requirements – CTI → IEC View Applied|https://www.figma.com/design/MB19XU18rQHSpba8pSsObn/Release?node-id=6859-63408])

The direction of the view is determined by the selected pair: if 'IEC62443 → CTI/CTS' is selected, IEC62443 requirements are shown as the primary structure and CTI/CTS requirements are shown as linked children. If 'CTI/CTS → IEC62443' is selected, CTI/CTS is the primary structure.

The category of a linked requirement is ignored in the hierarchy display: if a linked requirement belongs to a different category, it is still shown under its mapped requirement from the 1st standard.

Requirements from the 1st Standard that are not scoped for the release are not shown by default. They become visible when the 'Show All Requirements' toggle is enabled or when the requirement has been manually added to the release.

All requirements not related to the selected mapping pair are still shown on the page, using the standard sort order numbers from Backoffice. The traceability view changes the display grouping only — it is not a filter that hides unrelated requirements.

When a specific 'Traceability view' is selected, a 'Traceability focus' filter appears on the page with three options:
  - All requirements (default) — both mapped and unmapped requirements are shown
  - Only gaps (not linked) — only requirements from the 1st Standard that have no linked requirement in the 2nd Standard are shown
  - Only linked — only requirements that have at least one linked requirement in the selected mapping pair are shown (see Figma [Product Requirements – Traceability Focus – Focus|https://www.figma.com/design/MB19XU18rQHSpba8pSsObn/Release?node-id=7264-59726])

The Settings submenu Traceability view dropdown shows the filled/selected state when a mapping pair is chosen. (see Figma [Product Requirements – Settings Submenu Opened – Traceability view – Filled|https://www.figma.com/design/MB19XU18rQHSpba8pSsObn/Release?node-id=6789-73313])
Selecting 'All requirements' from the Traceability view dropdown restores the default flat list view, hides the 'Traceability focus' filter, and removes the hierarchical grouping.
The Traceability view selection persists within the session on that page. Navigating away and returning may reset to the product's configured default traceability view.

A 'Standard filter applied' popup state is shown when filters narrow down the visible standards. (see Figma [Popup – Standard Filter Applied|https://www.figma.com/design/MB19XU18rQHSpba8pSsObn/Release?node-id=7193-123301])

stories.title: Filter Product Requirements by Priority in Release
stories.as_a: user with access to the Product Requirements page or Cybersecurity Residual Risks page in a release
stories.i_want: to filter product requirements by Priority and see the Priority column on the Cybersecurity Residual Risks page
stories.so_that: I can quickly focus on requirements with a specific priority level
acceptance_criteria: A new 'Priority' filter dropdown is added to the 'Product Requirements' page in the release. The dropdown lists all active priorities from the Priorities LoV. Selecting a priority narrows the requirements list to only requirements assigned that priority value. (see Figma [Product Requirements – Priority Filter|https://www.figma.com/design/MB19XU18rQHSpba8pSsObn/Release?node-id=7286-13402])

A new 'Priority' filter dropdown is also added to the 'Product Requirement Summary' tab on the 'Cybersecurity Residual Risks' page. The dropdown lists all active priorities from the Priorities LoV. Selecting a priority filters the requirements table accordingly.

A new 'Priority' column is added to the product requirements table on the 'Product Requirement Summary' tab on the 'Cybersecurity Residual Risks' page. The column displays the priority assigned to each requirement.
When no Priority filter is selected (default state), all requirements are displayed regardless of priority.

The Priority filter dropdown on both pages reflects only active priorities. Inactive priorities do not appear in the dropdown but remain visible in the table if a requirement was previously assigned that priority.
The Priority filter can be combined with other existing filters on the page (e.g., Status, Category, Search) to further narrow the results.
Clearing the Priority filter (selecting the empty/default option) restores the full unfiltered list.
The Priority column in the Product Requirements table on the Cybersecurity Residual Risks tab is sortable and filterable consistent with other columns on that page.

confluence_links.label: Requirements Traceability within Standards – Adding Priority filter for the product requirements in release

stories.source_requirement: Frontoffice → Adding 'Priority' filter for the product requirements in release

stories.title: [In Question] Requirements Traceability View on Requirements Evaluation Page
stories.as_a: user with access to the Product Requirement Summary tab on the Cybersecurity Residual Risks page
stories.i_want: to select a requirements traceability view on the 'Product Requirement Summary' tab
stories.so_that: I can evaluate requirements from different standards side-by-side in the same hierarchical layout as on the Product Requirements page
acceptance_criteria: Upon clicking the 'Options' icon on the 'Product Requirement Summary' tab of the 'Cybersecurity Residual Risks' page, a 'Traceability view' dropdown is shown listing all active standards mapping pairs from PICASso.

Upon selecting a specific mapping pair, the requirements list is refreshed and linked requirements from the selected mapping pair are displayed in the same hierarchical structure as on the Product Requirements page:
  1. Parent requirement from the 1st Standard
  2. Linked requirement from the 2nd Standard (if any) — highlighted in blue with chain icon
  3. Sub-requirement of the parent from the 1st Standard
  4. Linked requirement for the sub-requirement from the 2nd Standard (if any) — highlighted in blue with chain icon
The directionality of the view matches the selected pair (e.g., 'IEC62443 → CTI/CTS' makes IEC62443 the primary standard).
Category of linked requirements is ignored: requirements from different categories in the 2nd Standard are still displayed under their mapped parent from the 1st Standard.
Linked requirements not scoped for the release are shown only when 'Show All Requirements' is toggled on or when manually added to the release.
All requirements unrelated to the selected mapping pair continue to be shown, using their standard sort order from Backoffice. The view is a display grouping, not a filter.
When a traceability view is active, the 'Traceability focus' filter appears (same as on the Product Requirements page): All requirements (default), Only gaps (not linked), Only linked.
This feature is 'In Question' — implementation is subject to confirmation from the product team before development begins.

Product Requirements – CTI → IEC View Applied

confluence_links.label: Requirements Traceability within Standards – Mapped requirements display for Requirements Evaluation

stories.source_requirement: Frontoffice → Mapped requirements display for Requirements Evaluation (Do we need this view here?)

questions.title: Process Requirements: Does Mapping Apply?
questions.detail: The spec opens with 'Do we need it for Process Requirements?' regarding the Requirements Mapping page. It is unclear whether the Requirements Mapping feature (view list, add/edit pairs, activate/deactivate, file upload) should also apply to Process Requirements or only to Product Requirements. This needs confirmation before development begins to avoid scope gaps.

questions.title: Requirements Mapping Tab on Edit Requirement Page: Confirmed or Not?
questions.detail: The spec marks the 'Requirements Mapping tab on the Edit Requirements page' section as 'In question if we need it'. Development should not begin on this story until the product team confirms it is in scope.

questions.title: Mapped Requirements Display on Requirements Evaluation: Confirmed or Not?
questions.detail: The spec includes 'Do we need this view here?' for the traceability view on the 'Product Requirement Summary' tab on the Cybersecurity Residual Risks page. Development should not begin on this story until confirmed by the product team.

questions.title: 'New Standard' Button Label on Priorities Page — Spec Error
questions.detail: The spec states that clicking 'Add Priority' opens 'New Standard' page — this appears to be a copy-paste error. The correct behavior should open a 'New Priority' page. Please confirm the correct page name/label before development.

questions.title: Deactivated Mapping Pair: Effect on Existing Traceability Views
questions.detail: The spec states a deactivated mapping pair 'is not considered for the view in release, when specific view is selected for the release'. However, if a release already has a traceability view selected based on that pair, it is unclear what happens — does the view revert to 'All requirements' automatically, or does it show an empty/broken state? Needs clarification.

questions.title: Deactivated Standard: Effect on Existing Requirements Mapping Pairs
questions.detail: If a Standard is deactivated, it can no longer be selected for Sources. However, it is unclear whether existing Requirements Mapping pairs that use that standard remain active or are automatically deactivated. The spec does not address this cascading behavior.

questions.title: Traceability View Persistence Across Sessions/Navigation
questions.detail: The spec is silent on whether the Traceability view selection on the Product Requirements page persists across navigation (e.g., leaving the page and returning within the same session, or across sessions). Clarification is needed: does the product-level default always reset on page load, or does the per-session selection take priority?

questions.title: Linked Requirements Ordering Within Traceability Hierarchy
questions.detail: The spec defines the 4-level hierarchy (parent → linked parent → sub-req → linked sub-req) but does not specify the sort order within each level (e.g., by code, by name, by order number). Confirm whether the Backoffice order numbers apply to linked requirements or only to primary standard requirements.

questions.title: Jama API: New Priority Values in MustOrShould Field
questions.detail: The spec confirms the Jama API field 'MustOrShould' remains unchanged but new values from the Priorities LoV will be sent in it. However, if a new priority value (e.g., 'Critical') doesn't exist in Jama's allowed values for that field, this could cause API errors. The team should verify whether Jama accepts free-form strings in this field or whether values must be pre-registered in Jama.

questions.title: Backoffice: Does the 'Requirements Mapping' Page Require Upload Permissions?
questions.detail: The spec does not specify whether all users with Backoffice access can upload files or whether there is a specific permission for file upload. Clarify whether the 'Select file' upload button and 'Add Requirements Mapping Pair' button are visible to all Backoffice users or restricted to specific roles.
